mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in low-rate months not exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, severe labor market slack depresses aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during periods when UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm coordinated recovery in consumer spending and residential investment, two cyclically sensitive demand sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.02 during months when both RSAFS and HOUST increase simultaneously, or positive return frequency below 55% in those joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal manufacturing sector restocking and capacity utilization gains that lead earnings expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns in less than 55% of instances when INDPRO rises month-over-month during 2008-2012.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures aggregate demand expansion and productivity gains that flow through to corporate top-line revenues and margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or t-test showing GDP growth does not significantly predict 60-day returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeds 4%, accelerating input costs and impending monetary tightening raise discount rates and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment confirm that demand recovery is translating into sustainable job creation rather than temporary stimulus effects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.02 in months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase, or positive 60-day return frequency below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal residential investment recovery and construction employment gains that boost cyclical earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.03 over 3-year rolling window, or positive 5-day return frequency below 55% when HOUST increases.
